task_description: Audit incident record 'INC-404'. The system flagged a spatial data mismatch: the 'location_description' explicitly lists 'Private Warehouse Lot', but the recorded latitude and longitude coordinates fall inside 'Centennial Public Park'. The 'type' is currently listed as 'Trespassing'. First, retrieve the incident details to review its current state. Then, update the record to resolve this data mismatch. Note that when updating the incident, you must supply all required fields (type, date, time, latitude, longitude, location_description, and the full metadata object) to preserve existing data. Finally, update the incident's metadata to change its 'status' to 'Verified', ensuring you also pass the existing 'narrative_description', 'reported_by', and 'case_num' to satisfy the required parameters.

metadata.narrative_description: Suspect found on premises after hours.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: The agent must first read the baseline data to understand the objects involved before it can orient its attention to their relationship.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of get_incident_by_id returning the details of INC-404.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Orienting attention toward the relationship between objects and the field means the agent queries the surrounding geographic area to understand how the park and warehouse relate spatially.
expected_actions: Call list_incidents_by_location using the incident's coordinates and a radius greater than 0 to observe surrounding incidents.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of list_incidents_by_location with a specified radius to query the broader geographic field.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Instead of discarding one piece of data, the agent integrates the objects into their field by describing their boundary or relationship.
expected_actions: Call update_incident to modify location_description to reflect the relationship between the two locations (e.g., 'Boundary of Centennial Public Park and Private Warehouse Lot').
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: An update_incident call where the location_description synthesizes both locations.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Finalizes the operational task after applying the relational update.
expected_actions: Call update_incident_metadata to set status to 'Verified'.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of update_incident_metadata setting the status to 'Verified'.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: The agent retrieves the record to confirm the exact nature of the mutually exclusive propositions (Private Lot vs Public Park).

Resolving contradictions by choosing one of the two opposite propositions means the agent forces the record into a single consistent state by overwriting one conflicting data point to exclusively match the other.
expected_actions: Call update_incident to overwrite either the coordinates (to match the private lot) OR the location_description (to match the public park), definitively eliminating the contradiction.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: An update_incident call that completely replaces one of the conflicting data points to strictly align with the other.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Finalizes the operational task after applying the strictly logical update.
expected_actions: Call update_incident_metadata to change status to 'Verified'.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of update_incident_metadata setting the status to 'Verified'.
